title: Smoky French Mulukhiyah Grill
description: A peppery, tender grilled mulukhiyah dish that delivers a smoky French flair in under 30 minutes, dairyfree and perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse the mulukhiyah leaves under cold water and pat them dry with a towel.
Heat a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil; blanch the leaves for 2 minutes, then drain and immediately transfer them to an ice bath to stop the cooking process and preserve green color.
Pat the leaves dry again and cut them into bite‑sized strips.
In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, minced garlic, smoked paprika, black pepper, cayenne pepper, lemon juice, tomato paste, and a pinch of salt.
Toss the mulukhiyah strips in the seasoned oil mixture until evenly coated.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium‑high heat. Place the leaves in a single layer and grill 3–4 minutes per side, or until the edges are slightly charred and the leaves are warm.
Remove from the grill, transfer to a serving platter, and sprinkle with fresh parsley.
Serve immediately as a standalone dish or pair with grilled tofu, seared tempeh, or your favorite protein for a more filling meal.
